A short, refined sailing experience for travellers with limited time — the Nile's essential temples, unhurried, in three nights.

Timeless charm meets the gentle rhythm of the river. This express voyage carries you from the island temples of Aswan to the monumental wonders of Luxor, with traditional sailing, curated cuisine, and a daily sunset ritual on deck.
Three nights aboard your dahabiya, sailing north with the current from Aswan to Luxor. The pace is gentle but the highlights are complete — Philae, Kom Ombo, Edfu, and the great monuments of Thebes.

Embark aboard your elegant dahabiya and settle into the calm of the Nile. After lunch on board, a guided visit to the enchanting Philae Temple on Agilkia Island, dedicated to Isis. Begin sailing toward Kom Ombo with the silence of traditional navigation — no engine, only water and breeze — and moor beneath a starlit sky.
Lunch on board — local & international cuisine
Guided visit to Philae Temple, Agilkia Island

Wake to sunrise views before visiting the unique Kom Ombo Temple, dedicated to Sobek and Horus. Sail on past palm groves and villages to Edfu, home to the best-preserved temple in Egypt, then continue toward Luxor via Esna with long, relaxed hours on deck.
Continue sailing to Luxor via Esna

An early start for Luxor's West Bank and the Valley of the Kings, where pharaohs were laid to rest in painted rock-cut tombs. In the afternoon, the vast Karnak Temple complex and the elegant Luxor Temple on the Nile Corniche, luminous as the day softens.
West Bank — Valley of the Kings
After breakfast on board, check out and disembark in Luxor, concluding your tranquil sailing experience.
Is this itinerary too short to see the highlights?

Not at all — three nights covers the Nile's essential temples: Philae, Kom Ombo, Edfu, and the great monuments of Luxor. It is ideal for travellers with limited time who still want the full dahabiya experience.

Your cabin, all meals featuring local and international cuisine, the daily sunset afternoon tea on deck, and the crew are included. Temple entrance fees, guides, and transfers are arranged for you and quoted with your charter.
Can we sail from Luxor to Aswan instead?
Yes. The same highlights can be arranged in the reverse direction to suit your arrival city — just let us know and we will tailor it.
Our signature "Long Cruise" — the classic southern route deepened with an exclusive excursion to Abydos and the magnificent Temple of Dendera.

A deep exploration of the Nile, combining the classic temples of the south with a rare pilgrimage to Abydos and Dendera — two of Egypt's most sacred and least-visited cult centres, often missed on standard itineraries. The schedule balances iconic sights with serene sailing.

Six nights threading the Nile's great temples, with an exclusive full day ashore at Abydos and Dendera. Sailable in either direction — beginning amid the islands of Aswan, or with the grandeur of Thebes — both reaching the sublime reliefs of the Temple of Seti I and Dendera's painted astronomical ceiling.

Arrive in Aswan and board your dahabiya. Your journey begins with the beautiful Philae Temple — a short motorboat ride to Agilkia Island reveals this Graeco-Roman complex dedicated to Isis, famed for its elegant columns and picturesque setting. Overnight docked in Aswan.

Set sail north to the unique Temple of Kom Ombo, dedicated to two gods — the crocodile-headed Sobek and the falcon-headed Horus. Continue to the historic quarries of El Silsila, the primary source of sandstone for Egypt's great temples, with cliff-side chapels and royal stelae carved into the rock. Overnight docked in Edfu.
The Falcon God's Citadel & Sailing to Thebes

Visit the incredibly well-preserved Temple of Edfu, the largest dedicated to the god Horus, with its massive pylon, courtyards, and intact inner halls — a highlight of Ptolemaic architecture. Depart for a full day of scenic sailing toward Luxor, ancient Thebes. Overnight docked in Luxor.

Cross the Nile to the West Bank: the legendary Valley of the Kings, the imposing Temple of Hatshepsut at Deir el-Bahari, and the towering Colossi of Memnon. In the afternoon, a special northward sail to Qena, the docking point for Dendara. Overnight docked near Dendara.
Pilgrimage to Abydos & The Temple of Hathor

A full-day excursion by road to two of Egypt's most sacred cult centres. At Abydos, dedicated to Osiris, explore the magnificent Temple of Seti I — renowned for its exquisite raised reliefs and the mystical Abydos King List — and the nearby Osireion. Then the stunning Temple of Dendara, dedicated to Hathor, with its famous astronomical ceiling, vivid column capitals, and rooftop chapels. Sail back to Luxor by evening.

A full day on the East Bank of ancient Thebes. Stand within the colossal Karnak Temple complex — a vast city of temples, obelisks, and the forest of pillars in the Great Hypostyle Hall — then the elegant Luxor Temple on the Nile Corniche, connecting the world of the gods to the city of the living. Overnight docked in Luxor.
After breakfast, disembark in Luxor, concluding your deep journey through Egyptian history and spirituality.
Embarkation in Thebes & The East Bank

Arrive and board your dahabiya in Luxor. Begin on the East Bank with the colossal Karnak Temple complex — its obelisks and the forest of 134 columns in the Great Hypostyle Hall — then the graceful Luxor Temple, beautifully illuminated at dusk. Overnight docked in Luxor.

Cross the Nile to the West Bank necropolis of ancient Thebes — the Valley of the Kings, the terraced Temple of Hatshepsut at Deir el-Bahari, and the towering Colossi of Memnon — before the special excursion north. Overnight docked in Luxor.

A full-day excursion by road to two of Egypt's most significant temples. At Abydos, dedicated to Osiris, the magnificent Temple of Seti I with its exquisite raised reliefs and the mystical Abydos King List. Return to the Qena area for the stunning Temple of Dendara, dedicated to Hathor, with its vividly painted astronomical ceiling, crypts, and iconic Hathor-headed columns. Sail back south to Luxor in the evening.
Sailing South via Esna to Edfu

After the intensive temple days, enjoy a full day of relaxed sailing downriver toward Edfu. Pass through the Esna Lock — a feat of modern engineering that regulates the river's flow — and watch rural Egyptian life unfold along the banks. Overnight docked near Edfu.

Visit the monumental Temple of Edfu, dedicated to the falcon god Horus. Sail south to the sandstone quarries of El Silsila, with the cliff-side Speos of Horemheb and royal stelae carved into the rock. Continue to the unique Temple of Kom Ombo, beautiful in the late-afternoon light, then a final serene sail to Aswan. Overnight docked in Aswan.

Visit the beautiful Philae Temple — a short motorboat ride to Agilkia Island reveals the Graeco-Roman complex of Isis, famed for its elegant pillars and picturesque setting. On the way back, see Aswan's High Dam, the Old Dam, and the Unfinished Obelisk. Overnight docked in Aswan.
After breakfast, disembark in Aswan — with the option to extend your stay toward the temples of Abu Simbel.

The inclusion of Abydos makes this uniquely comprehensive, offering access to one of Egypt's most sacred and historically significant sites — the Temple of Seti I — alongside Dendera, both often missed on standard itineraries.
How demanding is the Abydos & Dendera day?

It is a full-day road excursion from Qena (roughly 1.5 hours each way to Abydos) requiring an early start, but it is uniquely rewarding for travellers drawn to deep historical and spiritual sites. Comfortable shoes, sun protection, and water are essential.

Either Aswan to Luxor or Luxor to Aswan — the temples are the same, the order differs. Starting in Luxor lets you experience the grandeur of Thebes first; ending in Aswan opens the option to extend toward Abu Simbel. We tailor the direction to your travel plans.
Can the order of visits change?

Yes. This is a private, flexible charter and a general framework — your guide may adjust the sequence of visits, especially on the West Bank, to ensure the best experience based on river, lock, and site conditions.
The full sweep of the Nile aboard a traditional dahabiya under tow — from the Pyramids of Giza through Middle Egypt's hidden monuments to the temple cities of Thebes.

A rare twelve-night voyage along the river's least-travelled reaches, linking Cairo with Luxor. Traditional dahabiya cruising, gently towed by a support tugboat for the long passage, with daily international buffet dining and the signature sunset tea on deck. Sailing times are longer than conventional cruisers — that is the point: this is the slow, authentic Nile.

Twelve nights spanning Middle and Upper Egypt — the pyramids of Giza and Fayoum, the painted tombs of Tell el-Amarna and Beni Hassan, the sacred temples of Abydos and Dendera, and the grand monuments of Luxor. Sailable in either direction; northbound (Luxor → Cairo) runs with the current and is noticeably faster.
Arrive in Cairo and transfer to your dahabiya for embarkation, with a welcome dinner on board. Overnight in Cairo.
Visit the legendary Pyramids of Giza and the renowned Egyptian Museum. All meals on board, including sunset tea with snacks. Overnight in Cairo.
Sail from Cairo to Beni Suef
Begin your Nile journey sailing south through the fertile landscapes of Middle Egypt. Sailing: approx. 120 km · 16–18 hrs against the current. Overnight in Beni Suef.
Excursion to the ancient pyramids of Hawara and Meidum. Sunset tea and dinner on board. Overnight in Beni Suef.
Sail to Minya — Tuna el-Gebel & Ashmunein
Continue south to Minya, with visits to Tuna el-Gebel and Ashmunein. Sailing: approx. 145 km · 19–21 hrs against the current. Overnight in Minya.
Explore Tell el-Amarna — the Northern Tombs and the Tomb of Ahmose (EA3) — followed by the impressive rock-cut tombs of Beni Hassan. Overnight in Minya.
A full day sailing south toward Asyut, watching Middle Egypt drift by from the sundeck. Sailing: approx. 125 km · 17–18 hrs against the current. Overnight in Asyut.
Continue south to Sohag with an excursion to the magnificent Temple of Abydos, dedicated to Osiris. Sailing: approx. 100 km · 14–15 hrs against the current. Overnight in Sohag.
A leisurely sailing day toward Qena, enjoying panoramic Nile scenery from the sundeck. Sailing: approx. 160 km · 21–23 hrs against the current. Overnight in Qena.
Visit the beautifully preserved Temple of Dendara, dedicated to Hathor, before continuing to Luxor. Sailing: approx. 65 km · 9–10 hrs against the current. Overnight in Luxor.
Discover the Valley of the Kings and the spectacular mortuary Temple of Hatshepsut at Deir el-Bahari. Overnight in Luxor.
Visit the monumental Karnak Temple complex and the elegant Luxor Temple before disembarkation, with a farewell dinner on board. Overnight in Luxor.
Transfer to the airport for your onward international flight.
Arrive in Luxor and transfer to your dahabiya for embarkation, with a welcome dinner on board. Overnight in Luxor.
Discover the West Bank — the Valley of the Kings and the spectacular mortuary Temple of Hatshepsut. All meals on board, including sunset tea. Overnight in Luxor.
Karnak & Luxor Temples — Sail to Qena
Visit the monumental Karnak Temple complex and Luxor Temple before beginning your northbound journey. Sailing: approx. 65 km · 7–8 hrs with the current. Overnight in Qena.
Visit the beautifully preserved Temple of Dendara before continuing north. Sailing: approx. 160 km · 17–19 hrs with the current. Overnight in Sohag.
Excursion to the magnificent Temple of Abydos, then continued sailing toward Asyut. Sailing: approx. 100 km · 11–12 hrs with the current. Overnight in Asyut.
A full, leisurely day sailing north through Middle Egypt, panoramic scenery from the sundeck. Sailing: approx. 125 km · 14–15 hrs with the current. Overnight in Minya.
Explore Tell el-Amarna — the Northern Tombs and the Tomb of Ahmose (EA3) — followed by the tombs of Beni Hassan. Overnight in Minya.
Tuna el-Gebel & Ashmunein — Sail to Beni Suef
Visit Tuna el-Gebel and Ashmunein before continuing north. Sailing: approx. 145 km · 16–17 hrs with the current. Overnight in Beni Suef.
Sail from Beni Suef to Cairo
Continue your scenic voyage north toward Cairo through the fertile landscapes of Middle Egypt. Sailing: approx. 120 km · 13–15 hrs with the current. Overnight in Cairo.
Visit the legendary Pyramids of Giza and the renowned Egyptian Museum. All meals on board, including sunset tea. Overnight in Cairo.
Optional exploration of Old Cairo and the Khan el-Khalili bazaar, or leisure on board. Farewell dinner. Overnight in Cairo.
Why are the sailing times so long?

This is a traditional dahabiya towed by a support tugboat for the long Cairo–Luxor passage. Sailing times are far longer than conventional Nile cruisers — by design. The reward is the slow, authentic river and access to Middle Egypt's rarely-visited monuments.
Northbound (Luxor → Cairo) sails with the Nile current and is roughly 15–20% faster than the southbound Cairo → Luxor route. Both visit the same sites; we tailor the direction to your travel plans.

Very few vessels sail the full Cairo–Luxor reach. You see Giza and the Fayoum pyramids, the painted tombs of Tell el-Amarna and Beni Hassan, and the sacred temples of Abydos and Dendera — alongside the great monuments of Thebes.
A classic five-night Nile voyage blending awe-inspiring temples with the tranquil rhythm of river life — sailable in either direction.

Journey through the heart of ancient Egypt between the engineering marvels of Aswan and the monumental wonders of Luxor. Five nights allow you to explore the major highlights at a relaxed pace, with the option of a breathtaking side trip to Abu Simbel.

The classic stretch of the Nile, sailed at a relaxed pace. Both directions visit the same great temples — Philae, Kom Ombo, Edfu, El Kab, and the monuments of Thebes — with the order tailored to your arrival city and an optional excursion to Abu Simbel.

Board your dahabiya in Aswan and enjoy lunch on board. An afternoon tour takes in the island temple of Philae, dedicated to Isis; the monumental High Dam that created Lake Nasser; the British-built Old Dam of 1902; and the Unfinished Obelisk in the ancient granite quarries. Dinner and your first night moored in Aswan.
Abu Simbel Option & Sailing to Kom Ombo

Choose your morning: an early excursion to the colossal temples of Abu Simbel built by Ramses II, or a relaxed visit to Aswan's Botanical Gardens on Kitchener's Island and a traditional Nubian village. Sail north in the afternoon and disembark to explore the unique double Temple of Kom Ombo, dedicated to Sobek and Horus, before mooring near Edfu.
Edfu Temple & Onward to El Kab

Travel by horse-drawn carriage to the immensely well-preserved Temple of Edfu, dedicated to the falcon god Horus. Cruise on toward the ancient site of El Kab, enjoying the passing river scenery from the sundeck.
El Kab & Sailing to Luxor via Esna

Explore the archaeological site of El Kab, an ancient city of tombs, temples, and rock inscriptions. Cruise toward Luxor, passing through the Esna Lock — observed from deck — and past the town of Esna, arriving in Luxor in the evening.
Luxor — Thebes, City of the Gods

Cross to the West Bank for the Theban Necropolis: the Valley of the Kings, the terraced Temple of Hatshepsut at Deir el-Bahari, and the towering Colossi of Memnon. In the afternoon, the magnificent Karnak Temple complex and the elegant Luxor Temple, beautifully lit at dusk. Farewell dinner on board.
After breakfast on board, disembark at your leisure in Luxor, marking the end of your Nile cruise.

Board your dahabiya in Luxor and enjoy a welcome lunch. An afternoon on the East Bank: the vast Karnak Temple complex of pylons and obelisks dedicated to the Theban triad, and the majestic Luxor Temple in the heart of the city, beautifully illuminated after dusk. First night moored in Luxor.

Cross the Nile to the West Bank necropolis: the Valley of the Kings, the unique terraced Temple of Hatshepsut, and the Colossi of Memnon. Begin your cruise south in the afternoon, passing through the Esna Lock, and moor near the ancient site of El Kab.
El Kab & The Temple of Horus at Edfu

Explore the ruins of El Kab, an important pre-dynastic and Pharaonic settlement. Cruise to Edfu and travel by horse-drawn carriage to its magnificent temple, one of Egypt's best-preserved monuments, before continuing to the mooring at Gebel el-Silsila.

Discover Gebel el-Silsila, the ancient sandstone quarry with rock-cut shrines and royal stelae along the banks. Cruise to Kom Ombo to tour its unique double temple, dedicated to both Sobek and Horus, then enjoy the final stretch of sailing toward Aswan.

A full day with two options: the classic Aswan tour — the sublime island Temple of Philae, the High Dam, the Old Dam, and the Unfinished Obelisk — or an early excursion to the colossal rock-cut temples of Abu Simbel, returning to Aswan's highlights in the afternoon. Farewell dinner on board.
After breakfast on board, disembark at your leisure in Aswan, marking the end of your cruise.

Both directions visit the same temples; only the order and the sailing flow differ. Sailing from Luxor to Aswan is upriver and can feel more relaxed, and ends near Abu Simbel for an optional extension. We tailor the direction to your arrival city.
Abu Simbel is offered as an optional excursion by plane or convoy on the Aswan day. We arrange it on request and quote it with your charter.
Are entrance fees and guides included?
The boat, all meals on board, and the crew are included. Temple entrance fees, expert guides, and land transfers are arranged for you and quoted with your private charter.
The entire length of the Nile — thirteen nights linking the Pyramids of Giza with the temples of Aswan, the most complete dahabiya voyage we offer.

Discover the whole river aboard a traditional dahabiya, towed for the long northern reaches and sailing the classic south. From Giza and the Fayoum through Middle Egypt's hidden tombs to Abydos, Dendera, Thebes, Edfu, Kom Ombo, and Aswan — daily international buffet dining and the signature sunset tea on deck throughout.

Thirteen nights from the Mediterranean delta to the Nubian south — Giza, Fayoum, Tell el-Amarna and Beni Hassan, Abydos and Dendera, the monuments of Luxor, then Edfu, Kom Ombo, and the island temple of Philae at Aswan. Sailable Cairo → Aswan (southbound, against the current) or Aswan → Cairo (northbound, with the current).
Arrival at Cairo International Airport and transfer to your hotel or directly to your dahabiya for embarkation, with a welcome dinner on board. Overnight in Cairo.
Explore the legendary Pyramids of Giza and the treasures of the Egyptian Museum. All meals on board, including sunset tea. Overnight in Cairo.
Begin sailing south through the fertile landscapes of Middle Egypt. Sailing: approx. 120 km · 17–18 hrs against the current. Overnight in Beni Suef.
Continue south to Minya, with visits to Tuna el-Gebel and Ashmunein. Sailing: approx. 145 km · 20–21 hrs against the current. Overnight in Minya.
Explore Tell el-Amarna — the Northern Tombs and the Tomb of Ahmose (EA3) — followed by the remarkable tombs of Beni Hassan. Overnight in Minya.
A full day sailing south through Upper Egypt toward Sohag via Asyut. Sailing: approx. 225 km · 31–33 hrs against the current. Overnight in Sohag.
Morning excursion to the magnificent Temple of Abydos before continuing south toward Qena. Sailing: approx. 160 km · 22–23 hrs against the current. Overnight in Qena.
Visit the beautifully preserved Temple of Dendara before sailing onward to Luxor. Sailing: approx. 65 km · 9–10 hrs against the current. Sunset tea on deck upon arrival. Overnight in Luxor.
Explore the wonders of ancient Thebes — the Valley of the Kings, the mortuary Temple of Hatshepsut, the Karnak Temple complex, and Luxor Temple. Overnight in Luxor.
Continue sailing southward toward Edfu. Sailing: approx. 110 km · 15–17 hrs against the current. Overnight in Edfu.
Edfu Temple — Sail to Kom Ombo & Aswan

Visit the impressive Temple of Edfu, then sail to Kom Ombo for its unique double temple before continuing to Aswan. Sailing: approx. 105 km · 14–15 hrs against the current. Farewell dinner on board. Overnight in Aswan.
Visit the remarkable Aswan High Dam, the beautiful island Temple of Philae, and the fascinating Nubian Museum. Overnight in Aswan.
Disembarkation and transfer to Aswan Airport for your onward flight home.
Arrival and transfer to your hotel. Overnight in Cairo.
Flight to Aswan and embarkation aboard your dahabiya. Overnight in Aswan.
Visit the High Dam, the island Temple of Philae, and the Nubian Museum. Overnight in Aswan.
Sail north to Kom Ombo for its unique double temple, then continue to Edfu. Sailing: approx. 105 km · 12–13 hrs with partial current assistance. Overnight in Edfu.
Visit the Temple of Edfu before sailing to Luxor. Sailing: approx. 110 km · 13–14 hrs. Overnight in Luxor.
Visit the Valley of the Kings, the Temple of Hatshepsut, the Karnak Temple complex, and Luxor Temple. Overnight in Luxor.
Continue north to Qena with excursions to the temples of Abydos and Dendara. Sailing: approx. 65 km · 7½–8 hrs with the current. Overnight in Qena.
Sail to Sohag via Naga Hammadi
A full sailing day north past the Naga Hammadi barrage toward Sohag. Sailing: approx. 160 km · 18–19 hrs with the current. Overnight in Sohag.
A full day sailing north along the Nile Valley. Sailing: approx. 225 km · 26–28 hrs with the current. Overnight in Minya.
Explore Tell el-Amarna and its Northern Tombs, including the Tomb of Ahmose, followed by Tuna el-Gebel and Ashmunein. Overnight in Minya.
Beni Hassan — Sail to Beni Suef
Visit the rock-cut tombs of Beni Hassan before continuing north. Sailing: approx. 145 km · 16–18 hrs with the current. Overnight in Beni Suef.
Visit the Hawara and Meidum pyramids before sailing onward to Cairo. Sailing: approx. 120 km · 14–15 hrs with the current. Overnight in Cairo.
Tour the Pyramids of Giza and the Egyptian Museum. Overnight in Cairo.
Is this the most complete Nile itinerary?

Yes — thirteen nights covers the entire navigable Nile between Cairo and Aswan, combining Middle Egypt's rarely-seen monuments with the classic temples of the south, all aboard a single traditional dahabiya.
How does the towing work, and why?

For the long northern reaches a support tugboat tows the dahabiya, allowing an authentic, leisurely cruise over distances no sailing dahabiya could cover alone. Southbound (Cairo → Aswan) runs against the current and is around 15–20% slower than the northbound direction.

Both visit the same sites. Cairo → Aswan builds gradually toward the southern temples; Aswan → Cairo flies you south to begin and sails home with the current. We tailor the direction to your flights and preferences.
A four-night classic between Aswan and Luxor — the Nile's essential temples at an easy, unhurried pace.
Four nights sailing between Aswan and Luxor, taking in Philae, Kom Ombo, Edfu, and the great monuments of Thebes. Sailable in either direction.
We're finalising the full daily programme for this voyage. Contact us and we'll share the complete itinerary, tailored to your dates and group.
A leisurely seven-night sail along the classic Nile route, with time to linger at every temple and island between Aswan and Luxor.
Seven unhurried nights on the classic stretch of the Nile, with extra time ashore and on deck. Sailable in either direction.
A seven-night roundtrip from Luxor — sailing south through the temples and back, beginning and ending in the heart of ancient Thebes.

A seven-night roundtrip that begins and ends in Luxor, sailing south to the temples of Esna, Edfu, and Kom Ombo before returning through ancient Thebes — convenient for travellers flying in and out of Luxor.
The extended "Long Cruise" — seven nights threading the southern temples with the exclusive pilgrimage to Abydos and Dendera, at the most generous pace.

Seven nights combining the classic southern route with the special excursion to Abydos and Dendera — the same sacred itinerary as our six-night Long Cruise, with an extra day to savour it. Sailable in either direction.
A quiet place for stories from the river — what we cook, where we anchor, who we meet, and why we sail the way we do.

The Nile rewards a slower pace. Over the seasons we've collected stories worth telling — about the kitchen, the crew, the temples, and the small decisions that shape a charter.

This is where they'll live.

We'll publish a few times a season — no calendar, no churn. Letters from the boats when something is worth saying.

— The VEDA team
